title: ERC4337 execute / executeBatch / delegateExecute with returndata bubbling and storage-slot guards
explanation: Three execution paths share the same returndata-bubbling idiom: (1) call/delegatecall is invoked with arguments copied directly from calldata into the free memory pointer; (2) on revert, returndatacopy + revert preserves the inner error verbatim; (3) on success, the result is laid out as a Solidity bytes memory (length prefix at result, data at result+0x20) and the free memory pointer is bumped past the returndata. delegateExecute additionally wraps the call in delegateExecuteGuard which snapshots the owner and ERC1967 implementation slots before execution and verifies they are unchanged after — preventing a malicious delegate from rewriting ownership. codesize() is used as the output offset/length pair for call() because returndata is captured separately via returndatacopy; codesize is non-zero so it satisfies the call ABI without a memory expansion.
when_to_apply: ERC4337 smart accounts with direct execute/delegateExecute exposed to the EntryPoint or owner
when_not_to_apply: Accounts using only meta-transaction patterns (no direct external execution), or accounts with restricted target allowlists
